title: China's weakened crude oil
summary:
Main
This week, in the East, we discuss falling VLCC utilisation to China and record-high LR2 global tonne-miles in April. In the West, we look at the most likely Aframax demand scenarios after the TMX opening, and we explore the outlook for TC2 and Atlantic Basin MRs. By Mary Melton
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-05-16 101526.png | assets/2024-05-16_no1wtz13cs03vxx5tbkjtfmz691yzt_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_f8f708ad8bd0.png
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-05-16 101708.png | assets/2024-05-16_no1wtz13cs03vxx5tbkjtfmz691yzt_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_efa8e659cc50.png
China's crude oil demand is slowing down amidst declining refinery run rates among Shandong teapot refiners and the Yulong startup delay.
